Title: Pac-Man — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The most famous quarter-eater of the arcade age, brought home to the Atari 8-bit. This is Atari's own CXL4022 cartridge (1982) — the computer-line version rather than the ubiquitous 2600 cart, licensed from Bally Midway and Namco. A loose cartridge from a single-owner estate of Atari software; used, honest, and untested, sold as found. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Centipede — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Atari took its own arcade hit — the spider-and-mushroom garden shooter — and pressed it for the home computer as CXL4020 (1982). The 8-bit computer cartridge, joystick in hand. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Qix — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: One of the stranger and more beautiful arcade ideas — claim the field while the Qix coils and lashes. Taito's puzzler on Atari's CXL4027 (1982), among the more sought-after of the 8-bit computer carts. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Super Breakout — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Before there was much of anything, there was the wall and the ball. Super Breakout (CXL4006) shipped paddle-in-hand with many an Atari computer — this is that cartridge, loose and honest. Uses paddle controllers. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Atari Logo — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Not a game but a language — the turtle that taught a generation to think in code. Atari Logo (RX8032, 1983), developed by Logo Computer Systems of Montreal, runs from the console keyboard. A niche and quietly collected cartridge. Loose, used, untested; single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Typo Attack — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Learn to type, arcade-style, while invaders made of letters rain down. Atari's Typo Attack (RX8057, 1982) by David Buehler, on the silver Atari Home Computer label. Uncommon educational fare. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: KoalaPainter — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The software that gave the KoalaPad its voice — turn the tablet and the Atari 8-bit into a sketchpad. KoalaPainter by Koala Technologies (00315-001, 1983), cartridge only. A natural companion to the KoalaPad hardware. Loose, used, untested; single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Star Wars: The Arcade Game — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The wireframe trench run that emptied pockets in 1983, brought home to the computer. Star Wars: The Arcade Game (Parker Brothers, Atari 8-bit) on its silver label — one of the more desirable Atari computer titles. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate collection. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Donkey Kong — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Nintendo's first great climb — barrels, girders, and a gorilla — licensed to Atari for the 8-bit computers (RX8031, 1983). A strong-demand cartridge among collectors. Loose, used, untested; single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: E.T. Phone Home! — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The computer-line E.T., distinct from its infamous 2600 cousin — E.T. Phone Home! on Atari's RX8030 (1983). The label has gone dark and worn with age, and it is priced accordingly. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Missile Command — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Defend the cities; the sky is full of incoming. Atari's cold-war arcade classic on the 8-bit computer cartridge (CXL4012). Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Zaxxon — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Sega's isometric leap — bank over the fortress walls and through the force field. Zaxxon for the Atari 8-bit (1984), full-color label, a desirable Sega title. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Defender — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Williams' unforgiving side-scroller — save the humanoids or watch the world end. Defender on Atari's CXL4025 (1982). A demanding classic of the era. Loose, used, untested; single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Dig Dug — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: Pump them till they pop, or drop a rock and run. Namco's underground favorite on Atari's RX8026 (1982), well-liked by collectors. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Atari Educational System — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The master cartridge behind Atari's Talk & Teach courses — slot it in, add the console keyboard, and the computer becomes a tutor. Atari Educational System (CXL4001), uncommon. Loose, used, untested; single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.

Title: Space Invaders — Atari 400/800 8-Bit Computer Cartridge (Loose)
Description: The one that started the home-arcade craze. Taito's Space Invaders on Atari's CXL4008 (1981), the 8-bit computer cartridge. Loose, used, untested; from a single-owner estate. Loose cartridge (no box/manual); Atari 8-bit COMPUTER version, NOT the 2600 cart. Used and untested — sold as-is.
